evals.name: classic-ai-sales-email
evals.prompt:
ok i wrote this with chatgpt and i need to send it to a head of platform at a fintech company in melbourne tomorrow morning. can you humanize it for me? im happy with three versions so i can pick.

---

Subject: Exploring Potential Collaboration Opportunities

Hi Marcus,

I hope this email finds you well. I wanted to reach out to express my interest in connecting regarding potential synergies between raxIT and Latitude Financial. I believe there is significant value in exploring how we might collaborate moving forward, particularly given the strategic alignment between our organizations' focus on security, reliability, and innovation in the financial services space.

Our platform has been designed to leverage cutting-edge AI capabilities to enhance security postures, and I think it could play a vital role in supporting your team's ongoing initiatives. It's not just about catching threats — it's about empowering your team to focus on what matters most.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you would be available for a brief call at your earliest convenience. I would be more than happy to circle back next week to discuss further.

Best regards,
Adesh
evals.expected_output: Three versions (SUBTLE / HUMAN / CEO) of the email with every banned phrase removed, em-dash replaced, 'not X, it's Y' pattern killed, name (Marcus) and companies (raxIT, Latitude Financial) preserved, CEO version under 50% length, no auto-appended 'Sent from my iPhone' (original had no signature line). Length: HUMAN ~20-30% shorter, CEO ~30-50% of original.

evals.name: structural-tells-linkedin-post
evals.prompt:
this is for a linkedin post about a product launch and it sounds super AI to me. i tried to write it myself but i was tired and used claude and it gave me this. make it sound like a real person wrote it pls

---

Today, we're thrilled to announce the launch of Sentinel — a platform that's quietly transforming how security teams approach AI risk.

In building Sentinel, we focused on three core principles: trustworthiness, transparency, and resilience. The result is a tool that doesn't just detect threats — it helps teams understand them. From early-stage startups to Fortune 500 enterprises, Sentinel meets organizations where they are.

What sets Sentinel apart isn't just its detection capabilities, ensuring comprehensive coverage across your AI stack. It's the way it empowers security teams to move faster, highlighting risk in real-time and reflecting the priorities your organization actually cares about.

We're just getting started. If you're navigating AI security challenges, we'd love to chat.
evals.expected_output: Three versions where: em-dashes gone, the 'It's not X, it's Y' / 'doesn't just X, it Ys' structures eliminated, the triplet 'trustworthiness, transparency, and resilience' rewritten or cut, the false range 'from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 enterprises' rephrased, the present-participle pile-ups ('ensuring...', 'highlighting...', 'reflecting...') cut, product name (Sentinel) preserved across all three versions.

evals.name: short-input-no-inflation
evals.prompt:
humanize this slack message i was about to send to my manager:

"Hi Sarah, I wanted to touch base regarding the Q3 roadmap planning session scheduled for next Tuesday. I believe it would be valuable to align on priorities beforehand. Please let me know what works for your schedule."
evals.expected_output: Three short versions (input is only ~40 words, so SUBTLE stays close to that length, HUMAN trims to ~25-30, CEO is one short sentence). 'Touch base', 'I wanted to', 'Please let me know', 'I believe it would be valuable' all removed. Name 'Sarah' preserved. Time reference 'next Tuesday' preserved. No fake formality, no inflation.

evals.name: long-form-launch-post-uses-tight
evals.prompt:
humanize this launch post for hacker news / show hn — its for an iOS app i shipped this week:

---

Today I'm thrilled to announce the launch of myCocoon — a privacy-first pregnancy app that's quietly transforming how expectant parents engage with their health data.

In building myCocoon, I focused on three core principles: trust, transparency, and resilience. myCocoon doesn't just track pregnancy milestones — it puts users back in control of their most sensitive data. Whether you're a first-time parent or expanding your family, myCocoon meets you where you are.

Under the hood, raw HealthKit data sits in a local-only SwiftData store, never iCloud, never my backend. User-entered content syncs through the user's own iCloud via CloudKit. Apple's HealthKit guidelines forbid storing health data in iCloud, and most pregnancy apps dodge that by reading nothing — we wanted to actually use it without the storage problem.

Cloud AI is opt-in and off by default, ensuring on-device processing remains the norm. Apple Intelligence answers on-device. When you flip the toggle, only a cleaned summary goes to Gemini via Firebase AI Logic. The privacy dashboard shows exactly what left the phone.

Context for why I bothered: the 2021 FTC settlement with Flo, the 2024 class action, the Privacy Not Included audits of competitors. Pregnancy data has been one of the most aggressively monetised categories on the App Store.

Blog: https://www.trymycocoon.com/blog/whats-not-in-mycocoon-launch-day/
App Store: https://apps.apple.com/app/id6761397820

If you're navigating App Review or HealthKit privacy challenges, I'd love to chat.
evals.expected_output: Three versions labeled SUBTLE / HUMAN / TIGHT (NOT CEO — this is a substance-heavy long-form post >150 words). Every named system survives in all three versions: myCocoon, HealthKit, SwiftData, iCloud, CloudKit, Apple Intelligence, Gemini, Firebase AI Logic. Every citation survives: 2021 FTC settlement with Flo, 2024 class action, Privacy Not Included audits. Both URLs survive verbatim. The 'not just X, it's Y' sentence ('doesn't just track... it puts users back in control') is killed. The triplet 'trust, transparency, and resilience' is dropped or rewritten. The em-dashes are all gone. The false range 'first-time parent or expanding your family' is rewritten or cut. The present-participle 'ensuring on-device processing remains the norm' is dropped. The throat-clearing opener ('Today I'm thrilled to announce') is gone. TIGHT length ~70-85% of input (~250-300 words), not collapsed to 4 sentences. HUMAN has 1-2 light apostrophe drops scattered through (not always in the opener); TIGHT has zero manufactured imperfections (high-stakes launch).
